wind_direction_diagnostic.note: Outdoor games only. helping_factor = wind_mph × cos(angle_to_CF). Score column shown is weather_score (model output). If HR rate climbs out_strong→in_strong but score is flat, the wind effect isn't being rewarded enough.

temp_humidity_heatmap.note: Cells with positive interaction (cell rate > additive baseline) suggest synergy between temp and humidity; negative = the combo suppresses HRs more than either factor alone would predict. Outdoor games only.

temp_humidity_heatmap_historical.note: Backfilled from prior seasons (environmental factors only). Player rosters and pitcher matchups vary by year, but the physics of HR-vs-weather is stable, so this is a valid sample expansion for temperature, humidity, wind, dome diagnostics.

archetype_dampening.note: Rows = pitcher vulnerability quintile (1=most elite, 5=most vulnerable). Columns = archetype similarity quintile (1=low match, 5=high match). Watch the top-right cell (elite pitcher × high archetype match): if HR rate is high but avg matchup score is low, the elite-pitcher dampening is too aggressive when archetype signals a real vulnerability.
